description: Checked-in enumeration of every writer/placeholder retired by EPC-05/07/08's authority cutovers (R5: same-package cutover). tests/evidence-residue-scan.test.ts consumes this file directly -- the enumerated surface and the scan that checks it cannot drift apart. scan_roots/scan_extensions bound the sweep; each surface's own patterns must return zero hits outside its documented allowed_exceptions.

surfaces.surface: scripts/verify-sprint.sh direct checks/latest.json authoring via cp
surfaces.description: verify-sprint.sh used to cp its computed checks report straight onto checks/latest.json (both the normal-path report and the finalize-acceptance path). checks/latest.json is now materialized exclusively from the evidence ledger by src/effects/evidence/checks-materializer.ts via its single call site scripts/emit-verify-evidence.ts.

workflow_ensure_harness_surface (.ai/hooks/lib/workflow-state.sh, canonical mirror assets/hooks/lib/workflow-state.sh) used to seed checks/latest.json with a literal {} placeholder whenever the file did not already exist. A missing file is now genuine absence, never a placeholder this library papers over -- see the EPC-05 code comment left in place directly above the deletion site.

surfaces.surface: mutation-observed.ts continuous contract-verification report defaulting to the acceptance checks_file
surfaces.description: resolveContractVerificationTarget used to return checksFile: resolveChecksFile(repoRoot) -- the SAME path acceptance evidence materializes to -- letting Stop-time continuous verification silently clobber a frozen --prepare-acceptance bundle. It now always returns the dedicated CONTRACT_VERIFICATION_REPORT_RELATIVE ('.ai/harness/checks/contract-verify.latest.json'); resolveChecksFile itself is unchanged and kept for its own unrelated general-purpose role.

surfaces.surface: bash workflow_write_handoff's independent handoff/resume Markdown assembly
surfaces.description: workflow_write_handoff (.ai/hooks/lib/workflow-state.sh, canonical mirror assets/hooks/lib/workflow-state.sh) used to assemble both handoff/current.md and handoff/resume.md content directly via heredoc. It is now a thin invoker of the single standalone materializer, scripts/recovery-view-cli.ts. scripts/prepare-handoff.sh's own no-library fallback (fires only when workflow-state.sh is entirely absent, e.g. a repo not yet through adoption) legitimately shares the EOF_HANDOFF marker name and is an explicitly classified out-of-scope bootstrap, not this retired writer.

codex-handoff-resume.sh used to define its own local policy_get/safe_repo_file/active_plan helpers and assemble the elaborate resume view directly via heredoc. It is now a thin invoker of scripts/recovery-view-cli.ts with --print-prompt passthrough; zero independent content assembly remains. policy_get() is intentionally NOT used as a residue signal -- it is a common local-helper name legitimately defined by several unrelated scripts (ship-worktrees.sh, sprint-backlog.sh, contract-worktree.sh, plan-to-todo.sh, check-task-workflow.sh, heartbeat-triage.sh); active_plan()/safe_repo_file() as bash function DEFINITIONS are unique to the retired body.

surfaces.surface: scripts/prepare-codex-handoff.sh independent Codex-global packet splice assembly
surfaces.description: prepare-codex-handoff.sh used to splice a per-repo <!-- repo:<key> start/end --> section into the Codex-global handoff packet via an inline Node primary / Python3 fallback heredoc, with zero separate tracked file. That splice logic is now an additional output of the single materializer (renderGlobalHandoffSection / updateGlobalHandoffFile in src/effects/evidence/recovery-materializer.ts); prepare-codex-handoff.sh is now a thin invoker with --with-global-packet. The authoritative materializer and its standalone CLI duplicate (scripts/recovery-view-cli.ts, kept consistent by inspection, plus its byte-identical asset mirror) legitimately render this same marker shape and are the allowed exceptions.

workflow_ensure_harness_surface (.ai/hooks/lib/workflow-state.sh, canonical mirror assets/hooks/lib/workflow-state.sh) was an undeclared fifth writer: it seeded handoff/current.md and handoff/resume.md with a one-line bootstrap placeholder whenever those files did not already exist, reachable from unrelated event-logging call paths. Retired same-package under D9 revision authority, matching the EPC-05 precedent already recorded in the adjacent code comment. scripts/lib/project-init-lib.sh's own $target_dir-prefixed bootstrap lines (adoption-time scaffolding for a brand-new downstream repo, a textually distinct literal-path form) are the documented, unrelated exception.

resumeAvailable() used to read resume.md's raw text and re-derive availability by scanning for the legacy marker plus a '## Resume Prompt' header. It now delegates to resolveRecoveryEvidence(repoRoot).available (src/effects/evidence/recovery-materializer.ts, EPC-06/07 checkpoint-backed evidence reader, read-only import). The marker literal itself is NOT retired -- recovery-materializer.ts's LEGACY_RESUME_MARKER constant and its standalone duplicate in scripts/recovery-view-cli.ts still render it verbatim as a stable external-observable-contract string; only the act of re-deriving availability by scanning for it is retired, so the pattern below matches the exact retired call shape, not the marker string alone.
